69.98: The Cold is a Fearsome Foe!
69.104: Shape and Feel of Equal Import
69.105: Don't You Just Want to Go Wild?
69.117: Hate the Sin, but Not the Sinner
69.8001:
If your trainee can't run very fast during the final spurt,
she might be lacking Speed.
69.8002:
If your trainee often falls in rank near the end of the race,
she might be lacking Stamina.
69.8003:
If your trainee is having trouble getting to the front,
you might want to work on her Power.
69.8004:
If your trainee can't keep up during the final stretch,
she might be lacking Guts.
69.8005:
With high Wit, your trainee will find it easier to position herself
and make use of skills.
69.8006: You should enter in Daily Races if you want to acquire more monies or Support Points.
69.8007: Strengthening your Support Cards and Umamusume are vital to winning races.
69.8008:
The outcome of a race can be affected by the weather, how the field runs,
and at times even sheer luck.
69.8009: Increasing the level of Support Cards makes their effects more powerful.
69.8010:
Uncapping Support Cards and leveling them up further
will unlock more powerful effects.
69.8011: Mood Effect will also amplify the effects of negative moods, so be careful!
69.8012: Friendship Bonus makes Friendship Training even more beneficial.
69.8013:
Speed Bonus and other attribute bonus effects make
their relevant trainings more effective.
69.8014:
Training Effectiveness makes all training more effective,
regardless of type.
69.8015:
Initial Speed and similar effects let you begin a Career
playthrough with higher attributes.
69.8016:
Initial Friendship Gauge comes in handy when
you want to build friendship earlier.
69.8017: Race Bonus and Fan Bonus also apply to goal races.
69.8018: Race Bonus helps you obtain more skill points.
69.8019: Hint Levels increases the level of hints given to your trainee.
69.8020: Hint Frequency makes hint events occur more often.
69.8021:
Specialty Priority helps a support character appear
in their preferred training more often.
69.8022: Event Recovery increases the amount of Energy gained from events.
69.8023: Event Effectiveness increases the beneficial effects gained from events.
69.8024: Failure Protection makes it harder to fail Training with that support character.
69.8025:
Energy Cost Reduction makes training consume less Energy
when performed with that support character.
69.8026:
Wit Friendship Recovery increases the amount of Energy gained
from Wit Friendship Training.
69.8027: Friendship Training becomes available after a Friendship Gauge turns orange.
69.8028: Friendship Training is even more effective when multiple instances trigger at once.
69.8029:
When you slot a Support Card in your deck, events associated
with that card will be more likely to occur.
69.8030: Events of Support Cards not in your deck can still occur.
69.8031:
Keep in mind that you cannot use a Support Card
of the Umamusume you are training.
69.8032:
You can trade in Support Cards you don't want for Cleats,
which can be exchanged for items in the shop.
69.8033: You can check what skill hints you can get from a Support Card in its "Career events" section.
69.8034:
You can increase the Potential level of a trainee
with monies and various items.
69.8035:
The higher an Umamusume's Potential level, the more skills
she gets at the start of a Career playthrough.
69.8036:
Items that increase Potential level can be obtained by placing 1st in
G3 or higher races in Career.
69.8037: Collecting enough Star Pieces of an Umamusume will allow you to perform Star Unlock.
69.8038:
Performing Star Unlock on an Umamusume will improve her attributes
and increase the level of her Unique Skill.
69.8039: Increasing an Umamusume to 3★ or higher will unlock her signature racewear.
69.8040:
You can trade in unneeded Star Pieces for Clovers,
which can be exchanged for other items in the shop.
69.8041:
Veteran Umamusume can grant inspiration as Legacies
and participate in Team Trials or other races.
69.8042: Note that when you transfer a Veteran Umamusume, she cannot return.
69.8043:
The rating given at the end of a Career playthrough is calculated based on
parameter totals and skills acquired.
69.8044:
Veteran Umamusume can obtain Sparks corresponding to what they
learned and accomplished during their careers.
69.8045:
Sparks have different rarities.
The more ★ a Spark has, the greater its effect.
69.8046:
The more a trainee accomplishes during her career,
the more Sparks she can get.
69.8047:
Some Sparks activate their effects during the Inspiration event
at the start of a Career playthrough.
69.8048: Attribute Sparks will improve your trainee's parameters when activated.
69.8049: Aptitude Sparks will increase track, distance, and style aptitudes.
69.8050: Skill Sparks will increase skill hint levels.
69.8051: Race Sparks will increase parameters and skill hint levels.
69.8052: When a Spark for a skill your trainee has already acquired activates, stats will increase instead.
69.8053:
Legacy Umamusume can continue to grant inspiration
in as many Career playthroughs as you like.
69.8054: Legacy Umamusume can be borrowed from friends using monies.
69.8055: When selecting a Legacy Umamusume, their affinity with your trainee is shown by ◎＞○＞△, from highest to lowest.
69.8056:
Classmates, roommates, and family members will naturally
have a higher affinity with each other.
69.8057: You get a discount when borrowing a Legacy Umamusume from a fellow Club member.
69.8058: You might learn something by checking the Career info of your friends' Star Umamusume.
69.8059:
When Rushed, a runner panics and loses endurance.
Higher Wit can help prevent this.
69.8060:
The better a runner is at navigation, the easier she can weave
through rivals blocking her way.
69.8061: Speed is particularly important for sprint races.
69.8062: Speed and Power are crucial for mile races.
69.8063: A good balance of attributes is critical for medium races.
69.8064: Stamina and Guts are key for long races.
69.8065:
A runner's predicted performance is rated in the paddock
with ◎>○>▲>△ from highest to lowest.
69.8066: Going out for some recreation can improve your trainee's Mood and recover Energy.
69.8067:
You might be able to cure a harmful condition by enjoying recreation
at a shrine with your trainee.
69.8068:
I wonder if something might happen after the URA Finale if your bond
with Reporter Otonashi is strong enough...
69.8069:
It might be easier to receive inspiration from Legacies
who have strong affinity with your trainee.
69.8070: Huh?! Support Card Events can also increase the hint levels of Rare Skills?!
69.8071:
Information related to Umamusume is displayed on the school
bulletin board on the Home screen.
69.8072: Sprint races are 1400m or shorter, and mile races are between 1401m and 1800m.
69.8073: Medium races are between 1801m and 2400m long. Long races are over 2401m long.
69.8074:
Rain or snow can cause track conditions to worsen from Firm to Good, then Soft,
and finally Heavy, making it harder to run.
69.8075:
Races are divided into levels. From lowest to highest,
these are: Pre-OP < OP < G3 < G2 < G1.
69.8076: The higher the level classification of a race, the stronger the runners.
69.8077: G1, G2, and G3 races are called graded races, with G1 being the highest rank.
69.8078: A trainee can obtain a Spark for her Unique Skill if she is 3★ or higher.
69.8079:
Racecourses for the URA Finale are chosen based on your trainee's
most frequent race destinations during her career.
69.8080:
The Senior Spring Triple Crown refers to the following three races:
the Osaka Hai, Tenno Sho (Spring), and Takarazuka Kinen.
69.8081:
The Triple Tiara refers to the following three races:
the Oka Sho, Japanese Oaks, and Shuka Sho.
69.8082:
The Senior Autumn Triple Crown refers to the following three races:
the Tenno Sho (Autumn), Japan Cup, and Arima Kinen.
69.8083:
The Classic Triple Crown refers to the following three races:
the Satsuki Sho, Japanese Derby, and Kikuka Sho.
69.8084:
If you don't want to accidentally skip over unread events,
you can change the settings in your Options.
69.8085:
If you use Star Pieces, you can unlock new trainees
even if you haven't scouted them.
69.8086: Growth Rate makes it easier for a trainee to improve their attributes.
69.8087: The higher the hint level of a skill, the less skill points you need to acquire it.
69.8088: In races such as Team Trials and Room Matches, your opponents' skill activations will be visible as well. You can disable this feature in the Options menu if you prefer.

69.9062: Did you know? Her braid always has the same number of braids in it.
69.9063: Did you know? She writes down any slang she learns in her journal.
69.9064: Did you know? When she's bummed out, she gets clingy with her BFFs.
69.9065: Did you know? She's great at moonwalking.
69.9066: Did you know? Her most prized possession is the jukebox her father gave her.
69.9067: Did you know? She usually falls in love with things at first sight.
69.9068: Did you know? Red cars always get her attention.
69.9069: Did you know? She has a pilot license.
69.9070: Did you know? She loves looking through bright and colorful sales flyers.
69.9071: Did you know? When she's agitated, it really shows in her handwriting.
69.9072: Did you know? She's great at giving piggyback rides. Whoever she carries quickly falls asleep.
69.9073: Did you know? She keeps several identical pairs of shoes just in case.
69.9074: Did you know? She played at the seaside a lot when she was a kid, and everyone called her the "Beach Monarch."
69.9075: Did you know? When enjoying nata de coco, she gets indecisive over when to swallow the gelatin bits.
69.9076: Did you know? She often goes to tea parties organized by her fan club.
69.9077: Did you know? When asked about her dream as a child, her answer was "stew."
69.9078: Did you know? She constantly lies about unimportant things, but hardly ever about the things that matter.
69.9079: Did you know? She's saving her New Year's money to buy a motorcycle.
69.9080: Did you know? She faithfully writes fan letters to her favorite manga author.
69.9081: Did you know? At barbecues, she likes to eat the hamburger last.
69.9082: Did you know? She can't figure out if matcha lattes are "Japanese" enough.
69.9083: Did you know? Younger students help her post pics of her character-themed bento lunches on Umastagram.
69.9084: Did you know? She prefers her milk tea poured milk-first.
69.9085: Did you know? She's extremely ticklish.
69.9086: Did you know? She's been using the same hand mirror daily since elementary school.
69.9087: Did you know? Her secret pleasure is challenging herself to stamp documents with perfect precision, no smudges.
69.9088: Did you know? She listens to Chopin when she wants to relax.
69.9089: Did you know? She hates clothes that are tight around the neck.
69.9090: Did you know? She's incredibly picky about action movies.
69.9091: Did you know? She never buys an article of clothing unless she's tried it on first.
69.9092: Did you know? Her dream is to one day buy a 500-piece colored pencil set.
69.9093: Did you know? She buys her clothes online without much thought, so most of them don't fit well.
69.9094: Did you know? She's very knowledgeable about the rules of all kinds of sports.
69.9095: Did you know? When browsing clothes or cosmetics, she thinks of the people those items would suit most.
69.9096: Did you know? She's an excellent brewer of green tea.
69.9097: Did you know? She learned quite a lot about golfing from her father.
69.9098: Did you know? She's very good at catching dragonflies with her bare hands.
69.9099: Did you know? Miss Nya the lucky cat and Daruman the hair tie were gifts from her grandmother.
69.9100: Did you know? She's really good at making origami.
69.9101: Did you know? When watching TV dramas, she tends to criticize the characters out loud.
69.9102: Did you know? She's a pro at dealing with both hot and cold weather.
69.9103: Did you know? She's a purist about making takoyaki and cooks them on a gas stove.
69.9104: Did you know? She recently learned how to play "Umapyoi Legend" on the violin.
69.9105: Did you know? She can eat a banana in under five seconds.
69.9106: Did you know? She knows a surprising amount about deep-sea creatures.
69.9107: Did you know? Those who have experienced her piggyback rides say her back is comfortingly spacious.
69.9108: Did you know? She can catch, clean, and cook river fish all by herself.
69.9109: Did you know? She can beautifully gift wrap any kind of box in seconds.
69.9110: Did you know? She frequently watches broadcasts of Italian soccer games.
69.9111: Did you know? She doesn't like the taste of mint very much, but never refuses to eat it.
69.9112: Did you know? She can fall asleep absolutely anywhere.
69.9113: Did you know? She's really into watching solo camping vlogs.
69.9114: Did you know? She has a collection of the finest-smelling erasers.
69.9115: Did you know? She can pick up soft tofu with chopsticks without breaking it.
69.9116: Did you know? She can tell the approximate time just by looking at the sun or stars.
69.9117: Did you know? She can tell how a goat is feeling by looking into its eyes.
69.9118: Did you know? Her piano skills are on par with a kindergarten teacher.
69.9119: Did you know? She occasionally likes to visit owl cafes.
69.9120: Did you know? She keeps her eyes shut tight when getting a shot at the doctor's.
69.9121: Did you know? She buys bread from different bakeries depending on what type she wants.
69.9122: Did you know? She really admires tatami mat makers.
69.9123: Did you know? She's a black belt in aikido.
69.9124: Did you know? She can catch beetles like no other.
69.9125: Did you know? She can tell what shop a bento lunch is from just by looking at its contents.
69.9126: Did you know? She brushes her teeth for more than ten minutes.
69.9127: Did you know? She names each and every one of her plushies.
69.9128: Did you know? She likes the smell of liqueur-flavored candy and chocolate.
69.9129: Did you know? She takes the morning horoscopes on TV very seriously.
69.9130: Did you know? She knows a tiny bit about opera.
69.9131: Did you know? She has exceptionally strong teeth.
69.9132: Did you know? She loves kabuki theater and goes to see it often.
69.9133: Did you know? She has a unique sense for naming things.
69.9134: Did you know? She's pretty messy when eating hamburgers.
69.9135: Did you know? She's a strong proponent of using only the best sunscreen.
69.9136: Did you know? The longest she can sit seiza-style on her knees is only ten seconds.
69.9137: Did you know? When she uses eye drops, she opens her mouth for some reason.
69.9138: Did you know? She's not very good at slurping up ramen noodles.
69.9139: Did you know? Once she starts eating candy, she can't stop.
69.9140: Did you know? She's fine with wasabi but can't handle spicy mustard.
69.9141: Did you know? She has several favorite pool halls, both inside and outside Japan.
69.9142: Did you know? She can spend hours and hours staring into a kaleidoscope.
69.9143: Did you know? She can't watch movies about animals, because just watching the trailer for one makes her cry.
69.9144: Did you know? She loves to watch trains go by.
69.9145: Did you know? Her face only resembles her father's when she's angry.
69.9146: Did you know? She's really good at tongue twisters.
69.9147: Did you know? She's popular among foodies for her soba noodle blog.
69.9148: Did you know? On social media she only posts pics of clothes she's bought or her nails after she's done them.
69.9149: Did you know? On days when something good happens, she cooks dinner in a special clay pot to celebrate.
69.9150: Did you know? She almost added a revolving sushi bar to the cafeteria, but was stopped.
69.9151: Did you know? She can speak seven languages.
69.9152: Did you know? She once received a letter of commendation for catching a purse snatcher.
69.9153: Did you know? She makes all her own clothes.
69.9154: Did you know? If she has more than two cups of coffee, she won't be able to sleep all night.
69.9155: Did you know? She wants to give solo travel a try one day.
69.9156: Did you know? She challenges herself to jump over basically every puddle.
69.9157: Did you know? She's secretly proud of the fact that people often ask her for directions.
69.9158: Did you know? She's quite good at playing the trumpet.
69.9159: Did you know? She enjoys mochi wrapped in oak leaves so much that she could eat just about an infinite number of them.
69.9160: Did you know? She believes the secret to good luck is not relying on luck at all.
69.9161: Did you know? Her favorite hero is Tōyama no Kin-san.
69.9162: Did you know? She's studied French long enough to tour the country on her own.
69.9163: Did you know? Her singing voice is like her grandmother's.
69.9164: Did you know? Her dog Sapphy is said to be "like THE cutest no cap."
69.9165: Did you know? She's great at doing impressions, but she'll rarely demonstrate.
69.9166: Did you know? Many of her belongings feature a swan motif due to her favorite picture book.
69.9167: Did you know? She spends quite a lot of time fixing her white bangs every morning.
69.9168: Did you know? She's friends with a super popular Umamusume actress.
69.9169: Did you know? She's unwittingly been a lot of kids' first crush.
69.9170: Did you know? She puts small objects on her head when she's stressed out.
69.9171: Did you know? She had to get dragged to the doctor's office by both of her parents when she was little.
69.9172: Did you know? She's actually a connoisseur of grilling meat.
69.9173: Did you know? Her hair ribbon costs as much as a school uniform.
69.9174: Did you know? She talks very loudly in her sleep.
69.9175: Did you know? She secretly enjoys hearing about her younger sister's surprisingly complicated love life.
69.9176: Did you know? She somehow ends up squinting in every picture of her.
69.9177: Did you know? She's great at DIY carpentry... or as she describes it, her "ritual to guide lingering spirits to the dawn."
69.9178: Did you know? She always carries a pair of vintage glasses.
69.9179: Did you know? She likes collecting rain boots.
69.9180: Did you know? She starts licking her lips whenever she gets excited.
69.9181: Did you know? She has a license to operate small motor vehicles.
69.9182: Did you know? She cheers up kids who seem down by giving them piggyback rides.
69.9183: Did you know? She often leads animal therapy sessions (especially ones with cats), but receives the most healing benefits herself.
69.9184: Did you know? She loves little accessories that resemble feathers or wings.
69.9185: Did you know? She calls her childhood friend in Australia whenever something good happens.

3.1:
Each Umamusume has different goals.
This is your goal for the time being!
3.2: These commands let you progress through the Career.
3.3: Executing a command consumes a turn.
3.5:
These are the different types of Training!
Each one increases different attributes.
3.9: Take note of the Failure Rate.
3.10: The lower your trainee's Energy, the higher the Failure Rate.
3.11:
Failing a Training can lead to drops in attributes
and Mood, so be careful!
3.13: When Energy is low, have your trainee rest to recover it.
3.15: Next, select a skill to learn. Use the skill points you obtained earlier!
3.18: You're all set! Time to enter a race!
3.19: The races you are able to participate in are shown in this list.
3.21:
In the paddock, you can check the stats and
popularity of the other runners.
3.22:
Predictions of a runner's performance are denoted
by the symbols [△] [▲] [○] [◎] from lowest to highest.
3.23: The higher a runner's ability and Mood, the higher her popularity and the expectations placed on her will be.
3.24:
Of course, popularity doesn't guarantee a win,
but the more favored your trainee is, the more you can expect from her!
3.25: You can also change a runner's strategy in the paddock.
3.26: It's best to select a strategy that plays to a runner's strengths.
3.27:
You're all set!
It's finally time to start the race!
3.28: Congratulations on making it through a Career!
3.29: Don't forget to use up your remaining skill points!
3.30: And with that, the Career playthrough is complete!
3.31:
A trainee who has completed her Career will be evaluated based on set criteria
and then registered as a veteran.
3.32: Good luck! Try to aim for the highest rank!
3.33: A trainee who has completed her Career can obtain Sparks.
3.34: If you select her as a Legacy Umamusume, you will be able to use her as Inspiration for another runner and pass on her Sparks!
3.36: Make sure you praise your trainee for all her hard work!
3.37:
Usually, a winning concert is held after a race,
but since you're in the middle of practice, we'll skip it for now.
3.39:
Although it was short this time,
a Career is usually three years long.
3.40: Please treasure the time you spend together with your trainees!
3.41:
The top right symbols represent the expert predictions,
while popularity is representative of fan opinion.

63.1:
Every day is a busy day at Tracen Academy.
Select a <color=#ff9900>Career scenario</color> and tackle the problems
happening around school.
Finishing a playthrough will help your trainees grow!
63.2:
Work together with your Umamusume for three years in the <color=#ff9900>Twinkle Series</color> and <color=#ff9900>make their dreams come true</color>!
Every Umamusume has different goals.
Guide your trainees and be the ideal trainer they need to achieve their dreams.
63.3: Each Umamusume is unique! They all have different attributes, skills, and aptitudes. You can train them as many times as you like, so <color=#ff9900>how about you start by selecting the one you're most interested in!</color>
63.4: <color=#ff9900>Inspire</color> your trainee with the legacies of veteran runners! <color=#ff9900>Sparks of Inspiration</color> can raise attributes and aptitudes at the start of a Career, as well as during it through <color=#ff9900>Inspiration events</color>. Veterans can pass on their legacy as many times as you like!
63.5:
Let's <color=#ff9900>choose</color> Support Cards! You will receive <color=#ff9900>Support Card Effects</color>
from the characters you select, and <color=#ff9900>Career events</color> corresponding to the cards are more likely to occur.
Please note that you cannot select the <color=#ff9900>character you're training</color> as a Support Card.
63.6:
Support Cards have <color=#ff9900>types (Preferred Trainings).</color>
The effects gained when training together vary from card to card.
63.7:
If you raise the Friendship Gauge of Support Cards during a Career, then <color=#ff9900>Friendship Training</color> will occur when you perform their Preferred Training.
Friendship Training will improve your trainee more than usual!
63.8: Use <color=#ff9900>upgrade items</color> to increase the <color=#ff9900>Potential</color> level of your trainees, unlocking new skills for them to learn during Career playthroughs. Upgrade items can be obtained through Career playthroughs and Daily Races.
63.9: Use an Umamusume's <color=#ff9900>Star Pieces</color> to unlock her hidden talents. Performing a <color=#ff9900>Star Unlock</color> will raise her base attributes. At 3★, Umamusume acquire their signature racewear.
63.10: Use <color=#ff9900>Support Points</color> and <color=#ff9900>monies</color> to level up your Support Cards, unlocking powerful new effects and strengthening existing ones. Upgrade items can be obtained through Career playthroughs and Daily Races.
63.11: Use <color=#ff9900>duplicates</color> of Support Cards you already own to <color=#ff9900>uncap</color> them, increasing their maximum level.
63.12:
You can trade in duplicate Support Cards for <color=#ff9900>Cleats</color>.
Cleats are valuable items and can be exchanged for other items in the shop. The first time you receive a Support Card, it will be stored in the Support Card List.
If you receive the same card again, it will be put in your Support Card Storage and can be converted.
63.13:
Once you complete an Umamusume's Career, she will be decorated as a <color=#ff9900>Veteran Umamusume.</color>
Once decorated, she can participate in the Team Trials and other races, as well as inspire trainees in future Career playthroughs!
Note that once transferred, a Veteran Umamusume cannot return.
63.14:
Umamusume Star Pieces can be converted into <color=#ff9900>Clovers</color>.
Clovers are very valuable and can be exchanged for items in the shop.
They can also be used to unlock new trainees or Star Unlock existing ones, so think carefully before converting them.
63.15:
Lead Team Sirius and advance the <color=#ff9900>Main Story</color>!
You will be rewarded for progressing through the story.
63.16:
Encountering Umamusume and raising your <color=#ff9900>Bond Level</color> will let you progress through their <color=#ff9900>Umamusume Story!</color>
Bond Points can be increased by completing Career playthroughs and racing in Team Trials.
You will be rewarded for progressing through the story.
63.17: Form a <color=#ff9900>team</color> of Veteran Umamusume and compete against <color=#ff9900>teams</color> from all over the world. You will move up in <color=#ff9900>Class</color> according to how well you do! Once a week, you will be rewarded according to the Class you're in.
63.18:
Achieve excellent race results and record your <color=#ff9900>high score</color>.
Every week, the Class you're in will change based on your High Score.
63.19:
In Team Trials, <color=#ff9900>five races with different conditions</color> are held at a time.
As your Class increases, the number of Umamusume that can participate per race will increase, up to a maximum of fifteen.
Form a powerful team and challenge the Team Trials!
63.20: Build a team of <color=#ff9900>Veteran Umamusume</color> to compete in five races over different distances and surfaces. Be sure to choose runners with the right aptitudes for the job. The score your team <color=#ff9900>ace</color> earns for each race will increase by 1.1x.
63.21:
If you form a team of strong Veteran Umamusume, your <color=#ff9900>Team Rating</color> and <color=#ff9900>Team Rank</color> will increase.
You can also obtain rewards as your Team Rank rises.
63.22:
In Team Trials, you will play against <color=#ff9900>opponents with a similar Team Rating to you</color>.
The more you win against Teams with a high Team Rating, the easier it will be to obtain a higher Score.
Form a strong team and conquer the Team Trials!
63.23:
Take on a Legend Race with your Veteran Umamusume.
The better you place, the more rewards you will get. Tickets required to participate will replenish daily at 3 p.m. (UTC) during the availability period and when the Legend Umamusume changes.
63.24:
Take on Daily Races with your Veteran Umamusume.
Various rewards will be up for grabs depending on which races you participate in.
Your tickets will be <color=#ff9900>fully replenished</color> every day.
63.25: In Practice mode, you can set up races with <color=#ff9900>any conditions of your choosing</color>. You can enter not only your own Veteran Umamusume, but also <color=#ff9900>those of other trainers</color>. You can practice with up to 18 runners at once.
63.26: You can race again with Umamusume you've seen in Team Trials and Room Matches by saving them as <color=#ff9900>practice partners</color>. The Star Umamusume of friends you follow can join in too.
63.27: You can <color=#ff9900>share</color> your favorite <color=#ff9900>Veteran Umamusume</color> with your Club and on social media. Share and save lots of practice partners with your clubmates and friends to make the most out of practice races!
63.30:
<color=#ff9900>Join Clubs</color> with trainers from all over the world.
You can interact with other members and help each other train.
63.31:
Members can give each other <color=#ff9900>various items</color>.
Members can also borrow each other's <color=#ff9900>Star Umamusume</color> for Legacy usage at a discount.
63.32:
You can watch live performances of <color=#ff9900>songs</color> you have acquired.
Choose your favorite combination of main and backup dancers from among your Umamusume and outfits.
If an Umamusume is marked as a <color=#ff9900>Singer</color>, you can also hear her voice.
Songs can be acquired through the Main Story and Career.
63.33:
You can check and set your trainer info.
You can get <color=#ff9900>birthday wishes</color> from Umamusume on the Home screen, and experience slightly different lines in scenarios depending on your <color=#ff9900>gender</color>.
(Gender can be changed at any time.)
63.34: Choose a <color=#ff9900>Star Umamusume</color> and <color=#ff9900>Career Support</color> to help out your fellow trainers. Every time a follower borrows them, <color=#ff9900>Friend Points</color> will be sent to your Presents the following day.
63.35: Earn titles through <color=#ff9900>Title missions</color> and <color=#ff9900>events</color>. You can change your title at any time.
63.36:
Help make the director's dream come true!
You can obtain <color=#ff9900>trophies and rewards</color> by winning graded and Legend races.
In addition, you will be rewarded for completing trophy-collection missions.
63.37: You can now select the Star Umamusume and Career Support of other trainers you <color=#ff9900>follow</color>. Helping out a <color=#ff9900>mutual follower</color> will earn you more Friend Points. If you're looking for friends to follow, check out the <color=#ff9900>Recommended</color> tab.
63.38:
Collect records related to Umamusume to increase the <color=#ff9900>Umamusume Archive level</color>.
You can accumulate Archive experience by viewing stories and obtaining rating records.
Increasing the Archive level also earns you rewards.
63.39:
Try to get Umamusume plushies (also known as Cuties)!
You will be successful if you can get one within <color=#ff9900>three</color> attempts.
Succeeding may result in your trainee unlocking <color=#ff9900>new hints!</color>
63.40: Congratulations on your promotion to <color=#ff9900>Class 2</color>! You can now enter a <color=#ff9900>second</color> runner in each type of race.
63.41: Congratulations on your promotion to <color=#ff9900>Class 3</color>! You can now add a third runner to your team for each race.
63.42:
In Career, the aim is to perform well in your trainee's <color=#ff9900>goal races</color>!
To do this, you'll need to <color=#ff9900>train</color> and raise her <color=#ff9900>attributes</color>!
63.43: Learning <color=#ff9900>skills</color> will give your trainee all sorts of bonuses during races. The <color=#ff9900>skill points</color> needed to learn them can be obtained from races and some events.
63.44: If your trainee is running low on <color=#ff9900>Energy</color> or in a bad <color=#ff9900>Mood</color>, she won't be able to perform her best in training and races. Remember to be kind to your trainee by letting her <color=#ff9900>rest</color> to recover Energy and go out for <color=#ff9900>recreation</color> to improve her Mood.
63.45:
Umamusume may sometimes acquire <color=#ff9900>conditions</color> that negatively affect their training or how they are feeling.
You have a chance of curing these conditions by going to the <color=#ff9900>Infirmary</color>, so make sure to do so as soon as possible.
63.46:
You can execute various commands while training!
Execute them in a well-balanced manner to guide your trainee to fame!
63.47:
From the second half of June in her junior year, you can enter your trainee in <color=#ff9900>races</color>. Train well and aim for <color=#ff9900>first place!</color>
Depending on how she places, your trainee can obtain <color=#ff9900>skill points</color> and <color=#ff9900>fans</color>, which are required to participate in big races.
You can also obtain random items based on how she placed and how big a lead she had.
63.48:
Umamusume race for the first time in a <color=#ff9900>Make Debut</color>. If they finish first, they will be able to run in various races.
If they fail to finish first, try for a win in a <color=#ff9900>maiden race</color> from your next turn instead!
63.49:
<color=#ff9900>Attributes</color> such as Speed and Stamina gained through training are tied to <color=#ff9900>various aspects of performance in races</color>.
The <color=#ff9900>distance</color> of the race and an Umamusume's <color=#ff9900>running style</color> also affect which aspects are most important, so you will need to create a <color=#ff9900>training regimen</color> that suits each runner!
63.50:
The <color=#ff9900>track surface</color> and <color=#ff9900>distance</color> differ depending on the race.
An Umamusume's proficiency in a specific race depends on her <color=#ff9900>Track and Distance Aptitudes</color>.
Try to <color=#ff9900>win races that have a ★ next to them</color> in the race list!
63.51:
Races are divided into five levels: <color=#ff9900>Pre-Open, Open, G3, G2, and G1</color>.
To work your way up to winning at the highest level in the Twinkle Series, G1 races, start with <color=#ff9900>Pre-Open or Open!</color>
63.52: By expending <color=#ff9900>Energy</color> to perform <color=#ff9900>training</color>, your trainee can improve her <color=#ff9900>attributes</color>. Training <color=#ff9900>Wit</color> will instead <color=#ff9900>recover</color> Energy and also yield more <color=#ff9900>skill points</color> than other types of training.
63.53: Training with an Umamusume from your <color=#ff9900>Support Card</color> deck will lead to increased <color=#ff9900>training efficiency</color> and an increase in their <color=#ff9900>Friendship Gauge</color>. Once an Umamusume's Friendship Gauge becomes full enough, they'll start to trigger <color=#ff9900>Friendship Training</color>. Non-Umamusume characters in your Support Card deck may sometimes watch your training sessions too, which can lead to various benefits.
63.54:
When you see a <color=#ff9900>[!]</color> attached to a Support Card's character in training,
it means you have a chance at obtaining a <color=#ff9900>skill hint</color> upon successful completion of the training.
When a skill's hint level is increased, <color=#ff9900>the skill points required to obtain the skill will be discounted</color>.
63.55:
If you train while your trainee has <color=#ff9900>low energy</color>, she may <color=#ff9900>fail</color> her training and not improve her abilities.
Failure in Training has <color=#ff9900>various drawbacks</color>, so always keep an eye on your trainee's <color=#ff9900>Energy</color> and the <color=#ff9900>Failure Rate!</color>
63.56: By spending <color=#ff9900>skill points</color>, you can teach your trainee <color=#ff9900>skills</color> which will activate during races. Some skills only activate if your trainee is using a particular <color=#ff9900>strategy</color> or racing over a certain <color=#ff9900>distance</color>, so make sure to pick skills that suit her style and distance <color=#ff9900>aptitudes</color>.
63.57: Your trainee may gain <color=#ff9900>skill hint levels</color> from various <color=#ff9900>events</color>, including Support Card and Trainee Umamusume events. They can learn skills at a <color=#ff9900>skill point discount</color> based on their skill hint level for that skill, so be sure to use Support Cards that have the skills you're looking for.
63.58:
There are three main types of skills:
-<color=#ff9900>Unique Skills</color>: Special skills that Umamusume start with.
-<color=#ff9900>Rare Skills</color>: Powerful skills gained through hints from limited sources.
-<color=#ff9900>Normal Skills</color>: Skills gained through hints from a variety of sources.
In addition, there are also skills that can only be acquired through <color=#ff9900>inheritance of certain Sparks</color>.
63.59:
When you raise your Support Cards to a certain level, they will contribute to your <color=#ff9900>Support Bonus,</color> which <color=#ff9900>increases your team's score in Team Trials.</color>
Try and increase the level of as many Support Cards as you can!
63.60: You can view <color=#ff9900>exclusive stories</color> unlocked through events.
63.61: The midsummer Splash Recreation event is underway! Mejiro McQueen finds herself roped into the competition by Gold Ship, and with both unwilling to back down, the two face off! Meanwhile, competition is sparking off between Sakura Chiyono O and Maruzensky. Who will win the action-packed water pistol battle?
63.62:
<color=#ff9900>Train Umamusume</color> during events to obtain <color=#ff9900>Event Points</color>!
Collect a lot of points to obtain various <color=#ff9900>rewards</color>.
63.63:
By collecting Event Points, you can advance through the <color=#ff9900>Event-Exclusive Story</color>.
Advancing through the story and completing the Event Missions will earn you <color=#ff9900>event-exclusive Support Cards</color>,
as well as other rewards including <color=#ff9900>Rainbow Crystal Shards</color> and <color=#ff9900>event-exclusive titles</color>.
63.64: When <color=#ff9900>Event Bonus Trainee Umamusume</color> and <color=#ff9900>Event Bonus Support Cards</color> are in your collection, you get <color=#ff9900>bonus event points</color> at the end of Career playthroughs.
63.65:
You will receive <color=#ff9900>Event Points</color> and <color=#ff9900>Prize Coins</color> for completing Event Missions.
You can also get Prize Coins from the event's <color=#ff9900>Daily Missions</color>.
63.66: You can use <color=#ff9900>Prize Coins</color> to play the <color=#ff9900>Prize Derby</color>. Get a <color=#ff9900>bingo</color> to win <color=#ff9900>event-exclusive Support Cards</color>, <color=#ff9900>Gold Crystal Shards</color>, and more.
63.67: <color=#ff9900>Train Umamusume</color> and complete <color=#ff9900>Event Missions</color> during the event to obtain <color=#ff9900>Prize Coins</color>, which can be used to enter the <color=#ff9900>Prize Derby</color>.
63.68:
You can win <color=#ff9900>Prize Derby rewards</color> depending on how you place in the Prize Derby.
When you win a reward for the first time, you will have a chance to get bingo!
63.69: Fill in squares on the board as you win prizes, and receive additional <color=#ff9900>bingo rewards</color> for every line of three-in-a-row completed. For the first three sheets, you can move on to the next sheet after completing <color=#ff9900>eight lines</color>.
63.70:
Run as a team in the <color=#ff9900>Unity Cup</color>, which is held after the late June and late December turns end.
If you win, your <color=#ff9900>Team Ranking</color> will increase, and your <color=#ff9900>trainee and team members</color> will <color=#ff9900>grow stronger</color>.
Win more races in the <color=#ff9900>Unity Cup</color> to strengthen your trainee!
63.71:
Form your team and shoot to win the Unity Cup! Races are held for each division, and the team <color=#ff9900>with the most wins out of the five races will be crowned the winners.</color>
<color=#ff9900>Team Ranking increases with each victory.</color> The higher your opponents' ranking, the bigger the increase.
63.72:
By using Star Pieces, you can <color=#ff9900>unlock</color> Umamusume.
Once you unlock an Umamusume, you can start training her.
63.73: Register three Umamusume and <color=#ff9900>match with trainers from all over the world</color> to compete in real-time races! Train strong Umamusume and win against trainers just like you!
63.74: Choose between the <color=#ff9900>Open League</color> and <color=#ff9900>Graded League</color> as suits your play style. The two leagues have different limits on the <color=#ff9900>Career Rank</color> of runners you can enter.
63.75:
In a <color=#ff9900>Room Match</color>, you can enter your runners in races with other trainers.
- <color=#ff9900>Host</color>: Create your own race with custom conditions.
- <color=#ff9900>Join</color>: Participate in another trainer's race.
- <color=#ff9900>Spectate</color>: Enter a Race ID to spectate.
63.76:
You can hold your own G1 races. When you hold a race, you can choose from two modes.
-<color=#ff9900>Simple Mode</color>: This mode allows you to hold a race immediately.
-<color=#ff9900>Advanced Mode</color>: This mode allows you to set things such as the race's weather and participation requirements.
<color=#ff9900>Up to three Umamusume</color> per person can be registered to run in the race.
63.77: Each league takes place over <color=#ff9900>three rounds</color>. Starting from round two, trainers will split into groups A and B based on their race results. Advance to the <color=#ff9900>finals</color> and win it all!
63.78:
You can recruit participants for the races you hold from the Signed up Races list.
You can also use <color=#ff9900>Club Share</color>, <color=#ff9900>Social Media Share</color>, or recruit nearby Trainers by having them enter your <color=#ff9900>Race ID</color>.
You can also <color=#ff9900>save</color> races, so you can look back on your favorite races.
We highly recommend holding races with your friends.
63.80: Transfer Requests allow you to <color=#ff9900>transfer Veteran Umamusume to other teams in exchange for rewards</color>. Note that when you transfer a Veteran Umamusume, she cannot return.
63.81:
The <color=#ff9901>rewards vary</color> depending on the requirements of the team asking for the transfer.
In addition, each request has a rank, and <color=#ff9901>the higher the rank, the better the rewards that can be obtained for fulfilling it</color>.
63.82: Some requests with high demands even offer <color=#ff9901>Star Pieces</color> as rewards.
63.83:
When a special icon appears on a team member, you can activate <color=#ff9900>Unity Training</color> by training with them.
When activated, <color=#ff9900>the attributes of that team member will increase.</color>
Make good use of this to improve your team in order to win the Unity Cup!
63.84: Furthermore, if you participate in Unity Training with <color=#ff9900>two or more</color> team members, it'll <color=#ff9900>increase the training's effects!</color> Also, <color=#ff9900>the more team members participate</color> in the special training, the <color=#ff9900>greater the attributes and skill points gained</color> from the training will be!
63.86:
A team member who has performed Unity Training many times may experience a <color=#ff9900>Spirit Burst</color>!
When this occurs, that team member's <color=#ff9900>attributes will increase greatly</color> and <color=#ff9900>your trainee will also grow and receive skill hints!</color>
You should aim to frequently activate <color=#ff9900>Spirit Bursts</color>!
63.87:
You can check the items you have here. You can also use consumables. Some items can be converted into monies.
<color=#ff9900>Items that have been converted cannot be restored to their original state,</color>
so be careful when converting.
63.88:
As team members improve, your <color=#ff9900>Team Power</color> increases and you will receive gifts from Tazuna.
Additionally, as the team's attributes increase, so too will <color=#ff9900>training levels.</color> Since Team Power affects <color=#ff9900>how strong your trainee can become</color>, you should train your team members as much as you can!
63.89: Your <color=#ff9900>team title</color> represents an overall evaluation of your team's <color=#ff9900>ranking</color> and <color=#ff9900>Team Power</color>. The more prestigious your team title, the more your trainee will grow at the end of the playthrough. Forge your team's legend in the Unity Cup!
63.90: When starting a Career playthrough, you can choose to activate the <color=#ff9900>Event Boost</color> feature by spending twice the usual TP cost. If you do, the number of <color=#ff9900>Event Points</color> and <color=#ff9900>Prize Coins</color> earned at the end of the playthrough will be doubled.
63.91: You can <color=#ff9900>uncap</color> a Support Card using <color=#ff9900>a copy of the same card</color>. Uncapping raises the maximum level of the card, allowing you to further enhance it. SR and SSR Support Cards can also be uncapped by using <color=#ff9900>Uncap Crystals</color>.
63.92: Finish a Career playthrough in <color=#ff9900>Showtime Mode</color> to raise the <color=#ff9900>Show Gauge</color>. Raise it faster by completing lots of goals and playing on higher difficulties. Get <color=#ff9900>extra rewards</color> based on how many times you fill the gauge.
63.93: In Showtime Mode, <color=#ff9900>opponents</color> are harder at higher difficulty levels. Reach a <color=#ff9900>good ending</color> in a playthrough to unlock the next difficulty.
63.94: Train Umamusume with the <color=#ff9900>right aptitude for the race type</color> and fulfill the selected <color=#ff9900>score bonus</color> requirements to get the highest score possible.
63.95: During the <color=#ff9900>Trainer Aptitude Test</color> event, you can view <color=#ff9900>rankings of the Support Cards</color> most frequently used in each test!
63.96: There are no character-specific goals in this Career scenario. Instead, every Umamusume must pursue the <color=#ff9900>common goal</color> of <color=#ff9900>gaining Result Points</color>. Try to hit the required number of Result Points in each of the junior, classic, and senior years! You can choose for yourself which races to enter over the course of the playthrough, but you'll earn more points for more challenging races and better results!
63.97: <color=#ff9900>Reach every Career goal</color> to take on the <color=#ff9900>Twinkle Star Climax</color>. Beat the competition over three races and become the Twinkle Top Star!
63.98: Place well in races to get <color=#ff9900>shop coins</color> in addition to Result Points. Exchange your shop coins at the Pro Shop for training items, which can be used for a variety of useful things such as <color=#ff9900>raising attributes</color>, <color=#ff9900>restoring Energy</color>, and temporarily <color=#ff9900>boosting stats gained from training</color>!
63.99: Every year <color=#ff9900>at the end of the Late December turn</color>, an award is given to the <color=#ff9900>highest performing Umamusume</color> in each year. Make sure to deepen your relationship with supporters, achieve good results in races, and increase your number of fans! Winning this award will give your trainee big benefits, including increasing the level of her Unique Skill!
63.100: <color=#ff9900>Win against rivals</color> during Career playthroughs to <color=#ff9900>raise attributes</color> and <color=#ff9900>get skill hints</color>. Defeat the most capable opponents and train your Umamusume to the next level!
63.101: Place high in each of the three races of the Twinkle Star Climax to earn <color=#ff9900>Victory Points</color>. The runner with the <color=#ff9900>highest total points</color> wins, so work together with your Umamusume and aim for the top!
63.102: In Practice mode, you can set up races with <color=#ff9900>any conditions of your choosing</color>. You can enter not only your own Veteran Umamusume, but also <color=#ff9900>those of other trainers</color>. You can practice with up to 18 runners at once.
63.103: You can race again with Umamusume you've seen in Team Trials and Room Matches by saving them as <color=#ff9900>practice partners</color>. The Star Umamusume of friends you follow can join in too.
63.104: You can <color=#ff9900>share</color> your favorite <color=#ff9900>Veteran Umamusume</color> with your Club and on social media. Share and save lots of practice partners with your clubmates and friends to make the most out of practice races!
63.105: If there are Umamusume you want to practice with multiple times, you can save them to a <color=#ff9900>My Runners list</color> to <color=#ff9900>easily sign them up again</color>. You can even save them from the results screen after a practice race. Be careful when removing a practice partner, though—this will also remove them from any lists you have included them in.
63.106: If there are Umamusume you want to practice with multiple times, you can save them to a <color=#ff9900>My Runners list</color> to <color=#ff9900>easily sign them up again</color>. You can even save them from the results screen after a practice race. Be careful when removing a practice partner, though—this will also remove them from any lists you have included them in.
63.107: You can view the <color=#ff9900>top 100 scores</color> on all test types! The <color=#ff9900>top 30 Veteran Umamusume</color> are available for <color=#ff9900>unlimited borrows</color> in this event's designated training scenario!
63.108: You can earn <color=#ff9900>Test Coins</color> by completing tests. Earn even more coins by achieving <color=#ff9900>score bonuses</color>, <color=#ff9900>high ratings</color>, and completing <color=#ff9900>missions</color>!
63.109: <color=#ff9900>Certain items</color> can raise the <color=#ff9900>Hint Level</color> of an Umamusume's skill. Raising a skill's base Hint Level lets you start a Career playthrough with the level <color=#ff9900>already raised</color>. Give it a try!
63.110: <color=#ff9900>Get more points</color> in the Racing Carnival by using lots of skills and achieving great results.
63.111: Each time you enter a Carnival Race, it will fill the <color=#ff9900>Challenge Gauge</color>. Fill this gauge completely to start a <color=#ff9900>Challenge Race</color>, but be prepared—it will be <color=#ff9900>Very Hard</color> or even <color=#ff9900>Extreme</color> difficulty!
63.112: Place in the top five in an event race to gain a <color=#ff9900>Carnival Bonus spark</color>. Inherit the Carnival Bonus spark in a Career playthrough to receive the <color=#ff9900>Carnival Bonus skill</color>. Umamusume with the Carnival Bonus skill have <color=#ff9900>increased performance and likelihood of Great Mood</color> in event races.
63.113: When an Umamusume with the <color=#ff9900>Carnival Bonus skill</color> places in the top five of a race, their Carnival Bonus spark <color=#ff9900>levels up</color>. The higher the skill level from the spark gets, the more the racer's performance and likelihood of Great Mood will increase.
63.114: Use <color=#ff9900>Carnival points</color> from Carnival races at the shop to <color=#ff9900>get tons of useful items</color>!
63.115: The Daily Legend Races are a great place to test your skill—and get Star Pieces for the Legend Umamusume. Entry tickets are <color=#ff9900>fully replenished</color> each day.
63.116: You've got your very own <color=#ff9900>Trainer Card</color>, which can be used to share your accomplishments on <color=#ff9900>social media</color>. You can also <color=#ff9900>customize</color> it with a picture of your favorite Umamusume, Support Card, or in-game photo.
63.117: You can toggle whether to display your Star Umamusume's <color=#ff9900>Spark Sheet</color> on your Trainer Card. Export it for <color=#ff9900>social media</color> to show off your best racer!
63.118: Using the <color=#ff9900>Photo feature</color>, you can snap photos during both <color=#ff9900>races</color> and <color=#ff9900>concerts</color>. You can then spruce up the photos with your favorite effects.
63.119: Snapped photos will be saved to the <color=#ff9900>Photo Album</color> which you can view at any time. You can use your photos on both your <color=#ff9900>Trainer Card</color> and <color=#ff9900>Club Card</color>.
63.120: Place well in races to get <color=#ff9900>shop coins</color> in addition to Result Points. Exchange your shop coins at the Pro Shop for training items, which can be used for a variety of useful things such as <color=#ff9900>raising attributes</color>, <color=#ff9900>restoring Energy</color>, and temporarily <color=#ff9900>boosting stats gained from training</color>!
63.121: On a first Career playthrough with an Umamusume, the <color=#ff9900>best scenario</color> to get to know them better is <color=#ff9900>"The Beginning! URA Finale!"</color> Be the best trainer you can be as you experience the challenges of fulfilling your Umamusume's goals. Grow together, and go for the top of the URA Finale!
63.122: This scenario features many unique Career events that <color=#ff9900>can't be seen anywhere else</color>! Discover new sides of your favorite Umamusume through glimpses into their everyday lives, or just enjoy the ups and downs of the passing seasons together!
63.123: In the <color=#ff9900>All Together! Top Umadol Project</color> event, rewards for everyone are unlocked based on the total number of fans collected by all players in Career mode. Individual rewards are unlocked based on how many fans you personally collected.
63.124: <color=#ff9900>Golshi Mode</color> makes <color=#ff9900>opposing racers</color> tougher in Career races. Finish a Career playthrough in Golshi Mode to gain bonus rewards based on how successful the playthrough was.
63.125: Community Rewards are unlocked based on <color=#ff9900>the combined number of fans collected by all trainers during the event</color>. The higher the total gets, the better the rewards!
63.126:
In a <color=#ff9900>Room Match</color>, you can enter your runners in races with other trainers.
- <color=#ff9900>Host</color>: Create your own race with custom conditions.
- <color=#ff9900>Join</color>: Participate in another trainer's race.
- <color=#ff9900>Spectate</color>: Enter a Race ID to spectate.
63.127:
You can hold your own G1 races. When you hold a race, you can choose from two modes.
-<color=#ff9900>Simple Mode</color>: This mode allows you to hold a race immediately.
-<color=#ff9900>Advanced Mode</color>: This mode allows you to set things such as the race's weather and participation requirements.
<color=#ff9900>Up to three Umamusume</color> per person can be registered to run in the race.
63.128:
You can recruit participants for the races you hold from the Signed up Races list.
You can also use <color=#ff9900>Club Share</color>, <color=#ff9900>Social Media Share</color>, or recruit nearby Trainers by having them enter your <color=#ff9900>Race ID</color>.
You can also <color=#ff9900>save</color> races, so you can look back on your favorite races.
We highly recommend holding races with your friends.
63.129: If there are Umamusume you use frequently in Room Matches, you can register them to a <color=#ff9900>team slot</color> to <color=#ff9900>easily sign them up again</color>. You can even register them from the results screen after a Room Match race.
63.130: If there are Umamusume you use frequently in Room Matches, you can register them to a <color=#ff9900>team slot</color> to <color=#ff9900>easily sign them up again</color>. You can even register them from the results screen after a Room Match race.
63.131: You can change the outfits of Umamusume you've scouted! Aside from certain scenes that override outfit selection, the individual settings for <color=#ff9900>each Umamusume</color> apply throughout the game—even for <color=#ff9900>Veteran Umamusume</color>.
63.132: You can choose to add an Event Boost on the confirmation screen before starting a Career playthrough. With the boost enabled, the playthrough costs <color=#ff9900>twice the TP</color> and yields <color=#ff9900>twice the Show Gauge</color> results as a regular run.
63.133:
After the four Promo Concerts, a <color=#ff9900>Grand Concert</color> will be held!
By <color=#ff9900>learning songs</color> and getting pumped for their next grand performance, your Umamusume will experience exceptional growth!
63.134: There are<color=#ff9900> five different types of performance points</color> that you can acquire through training! The performance type in which you can acquire points through each training session will differ each turn.
63.135: The performance points you can obtain will increase depending on the<color=#ff9900> level of your training</color> and the<color=#ff9900> number of Umamusume participating</color>. In addition, if <color=#ff9900>Friendship Training</color> is successfully triggered, you can obtain <color=#ff9900>two types of performance points</color>!
63.136: The performance points you earn can be used to <color=#ff9900>learn songs and concert techniques</color> in <color=#ff9900>lessons</color>. When you learn songs and concert techniques, <color=#ff9900>your trainee's abilities will grow</color>!
63.137: In <color=#ff9900>lessons</color>, you can <color=#ff9900>learn songs and concert techniques</color> by using performance points! In addition to gaining <color=#ff9900>Mastery Bonuses</color> that take effect immediately when you learn a song, you can also gain <color=#ff9900>Concert Bonuses</color> that take effect after the concert and last until the end of the Career!
63.138: If you don't have enough performance points to learn something, you can <color=#ff9900>schedule it for later</color>. When you add a lesson to your schedule, the<color=#ff9900> remaining performance points</color> you need to learn it will be displayed on the top of the training screen.
63.139: Each time you learn a song, your <color=#ff9900>Hype Level</color> will increase, and if the gauge is filled to the maximum, your next concert will be a <color=#ff9900>Great Success</color>. Since the Hype Level is reset each time a concert is held, you should learn new songs in preparation for each concert!
63.140: <color=#ff9900>Concerts</color> are performed with <color=#ff9900>songs you have learned</color>. Perform enough newly learned songs, and the concert <color=#ff9900>will be a Great Success</color>. Track your progress towards Great Success by checking your Hype Level! If your concert is a Great Success, your <color=#ff9900>Umamusume will grow even more</color>.
63.141: If you finish a concert with any newly learned songs, their corresponding <color=#ff9900>Concert Bonuses</color> will be activated starting next turn. Concert Bonuses of the same type will have their <color=#ff9900>effects added together</color>.
63.142: <color=#ff9901>StarHorse4 Special Transfers</color> allow you to transfer Veteran Umamusume to other teams in exchange for rewards. Note that when you transfer a Veteran Umamusume, she cannot return.
63.143: The teams requesting transfers have <color=#ff9901>ranks</color> based on their requirements. The higher the rank, the better the rewards that can be obtained for fulfilling the request. There's even a <color=#ff9901>certain trainer</color> with a <color=#ff9901>rank of 4</color>...
63.144: Rank 3 requests offer <color=#ff9901>the transferred Veteran Umamusume's Star Pieces</color> as a reward. Requests that are level 3 or higher also offer <color=#ff9901>Star Fruit</color>, a new item for <color=#ff9901>StarHorse4</color> Special Transfers.
63.145: <color=#ff9900>Scout Races</color> are entered in teams of seven. The captain position is reserved for your Veteran Umamusume, while the other six slots are filled through <color=#ff9900>scouting</color>. Keep racing and scouting to make your team the best it can be!
63.146: The number of <color=#ff9900>Scout Race Tickets</color> is replenished to 5 each day at 22:00 (UTC), but they can also be replenished faster with items and daily missions. The <color=#ff9900>race conditions</color> change <color=#ff9900>every 3 races</color>. The upcoming race conditions can be checked from the Upcoming Race screen.
63.147: <color=#ff9900>Scout Points</color> are obtained from Scout Races. The amount earned changes based on race result, the opposing team's rank, and bonuses. Assemble a full team and tackle various race conditions to claim victory.
63.148: In <color=#ff9900>Scout Time</color>, you can <color=#ff9900>scout</color> Umamusume from the opposing team and swap them for members of your own team. The amount of <color=#ff9900>Scout Points</color> needed varies based on the Umamusume's rank.
63.149: Improve your team's stats to raise the <color=#ff9900>Team Rank</color>. Both raising the rank and completing <color=#ff9900>special missions</color> will earn you rewards.
63.150: If you win a <color=#ff9900>Scout Race</color>, the <color=#ff9900>winning</color> captain and team members will be<color=#ff9900> registered on the roster</color>, and you will receive a bonus to earned Scout Points and team members' base abilities. Furthermore, if you win a Scout Race with a specific combination of Umamusume on your team, you can acquire<color=#ff9900> Team Skills</color>.
63.151: <color=#ff9900>Event Boost</color> doubles the <color=#ff9900>Test Coins</color> you can earn after completing training by spending twice the TP at the start of training.
63.152:
You can fill up the <color=#ff9900>Outing gauge</color> by playing <color=#ff9900>each type of content</color> and by getting a <color=#ff9900>Outing bonus</color> for logging in. When the gauge is filled to the<color=#ff9900> maximum</color>, you can go<color=#ff9900> on an outing</color> with an Umamusume.
*If you go on an outing after earning the login Outing bonus, you will be able to fill up the Outing gauge by playing each type of content.
63.153: In the Photo Studio, you can take<color=#ff9900> photos</color> in various situations, such as<color=#ff9900> Training</color> and<color=#ff9900> Recreation</color>.
63.154: After selecting the Umamusume you want to take a photo of, set things like the<color=#ff9900> season and destination</color>. You can also set a partner when taking<color=#ff9900> Training</color> photos.
63.155: When taking a photo, you can toggle whether to <color=#ff9900>pause or loop the scene</color>. Try switching around to capture your favorite moments, then customize them to your liking with <color=#ff9900>filters</color>.
63.156: Going on an <color=#ff9900>outing</color> raises the Umamusume's <color=#ff9900>Bond Level</color>. Outings during Uma Outing events also may yield <color=#ff9900>unique animations and event rewards</color>. To rewatch a previously seen animation, tap and hold the Umamusume's icon during the event period or head to the Videos tab of the Umamusume's archive.
63.157: On the "Sparkly Magic Show" event page, you can find information about the Holiday Present Event, and you can also participate in the Tokyo Mega Illumi Collaboration Event starting on MM/DD. You can also earn rewards by tapping on the <color=#ff9900>petit gift that appears once a day</color>.
63.158: The Umamusume visit Oi Racecourse for a holiday collaboration with the Tokyo Mega Illumi. Upon arriving, however, they learn that the Mega Tree isn't fully decorated yet! The Umamusume decide to use all their power to help get the tree to its full sparkly glory lightning-fast! ☆
63.159: <color=#ff9900>Happy Meek</color> will now appear in training. Let's all work hard together during these three years to prep for the URA Finals!
63.160: After training with Happy Meek, there's a chance of starting a <color=#ff9900>competition</color> between her and your trainee. The higher your trainee's chosen stat is, the more likely they are to win, so train up and pick wisely!
63.161: If your trainee beats Happy Meek enough times during training, it'll push Happy Meek to train extra hard to be a <color=#ff9900>more formidable opponent</color> in the URA Finals. Keep on training to stay on top in the big race!
63.162: When activating Unity Training with a team member that's already experienced a Spirit Burst, there's a chance that they'll activate an <color=#ff9900>Extreme Spirit Burst</color>! While it can only be done once, the Extreme Spirit Burst will <color=#ff9900>raise the member's attributes</color> by an extra amount and <color=#ff9900>give your trainee a skill hint</color>! Use these Extreme opportunities to your team's advantage!
63.163: Meeting <color=#ff9900>special conditions</color> will cause<color=#ff9900> powerful teams</color> to appear in the Unity Cup! If you defeat a powerful team,<color=#ff9900> your trainee will grow a lot</color>! Furthermore,<color=#ff9900> something might happen</color> in the Unity Cup finals...!
63.164: The Tokyo Mega Illumi collaboration event “Sparkly Magic Show” is an event where you can earn rewards by collecting<color=#ff9900> ornaments to decorate the tree</color> through missions and events. You can earn<color=#ff9900> individual event rewards</color> based on the number of<color=#ff9900> ornaments you collect</color>.
63.165: With so many trainers pitching in, the Mega Tree is sure to shine! The <color=#ff9900>tree's appearance</color> on the event page will change based on the <color=#ff9900>total number of ornaments</color> collected. If you and your fellow trainers collect enough, the event rewards will get a glow-up too!
63.166: In this scenario, you'll have the support of none other than the Three Goddesses as you guide your trainee through her own unique set of career goals. Overcome your first three years together for a chance to challenge the Goddesses themselves in the <color=#ff9900>Grandmasters</color>!
63.167: Get an <color=#ff9900>Insight Fragment</color> every time you train, rest, go for recreation, or race. Once acquired, Insight Fragments passively grant you <color=#ff9900>increased attribute gain</color> from training and other helpful bonuses! And keep an eye out for <color=#ff9900>Friendship Training</color>, which will get you two Fragments at once!
63.168: Two Insight Fragments paired together grants you an <color=#ff9900>Insight Crystal</color>, which will further increase your attribute gain through training with its own passive bonus. The original Fragment pair's bonuses <color=#ff9900>remain active</color> too, so collect as many as you can to supercharge your training!
63.169: The Insight Tree houses all the Fragments and Crystals you obtain. Fill it up to acquire <color=#ff9900>Divine Wisdom</color>, which can then be absorbed at any time for a powerful <color=#ff9900>one-turn buff</color>. The effects differ depending on which Goddess you receive Wisdom from, so be sure to check the Tree for details!
63.170: A <color=#ff9900>special race</color> will be held <color=#ff9900>after your late December turn every year</color>, culminating in your trainee's ultimate goal race, the Grandmasters. Make full use of the Goddesses' power to prepare your trainee for these challenges!
63.171: Grandmasters is a special one-race event where your trainee will face off against the ultimate rivals: <color=#ff9900>Darley Arabian, Godolphin Barb, and Byerley Turk</color>—all three of the Goddesses! Train hard to win this ultimate honor with your partner!
63.172: This event consists of a <color=#ff9900>Main Stage</color> followed by an <color=#ff9900>Extra Stage</color>. In the former, you'll send a <color=#ff9900>team of 3</color> off to the races and earn <color=#ff9900>League Score</color> based on their performance. The more League Score you acquire, the higher your <color=#ff9900>League Rank</color> will rise, and the more rewards you'll earn!
63.173: <color=#ff9901>StarHorse4 Special Transfers</color> allow you to transfer Veteran Umamusume to other teams in exchange for rewards. Note that when you transfer a Veteran Umamusume, she cannot return.
63.174: The teams requesting transfers have <color=#ff9901>ranks</color> based on their requirements. The higher the rank, the better the rewards that can be obtained for fulfilling the request. There's even a <color=#ff9901>certain trainer</color> with a <color=#ff9901>rank of 4</color>...
63.175: Rank 3 requests offer <color=#ff9901>the transferred Veteran Umamusume's Star Pieces</color> as a reward. Requests that are level 3 or higher also offer <color=#ff9901>Star Fruit</color>, a new item for <color=#ff9901>StarHorse4</color> Special Transfers.
63.176: Fill the <color=#ff9900>Research Gauge</color> by playing a variety of content. Once full, simply go to the event home page to <color=#ff9900>reap rewards</color> based on how many times you filled the gauge!
63.177: Fill the <color=#ff9900>Bloom Gauge</color> with the daily <color=#ff9900>Full Bloom Login Bonus</color> to <color=#ff9900>move one space</color> on the event screen. After claiming your free move, you can also fill the gauge by completing Career playthroughs or competing in Team Trials.
63.178: Every time you move on the map, you'll unlock a <color=#ff9900>story episode</color> and earn rewards. New areas will unlock on the second and third days of the event at 22:00 (UTC). Once you're out of areas to visit, the gauge will no longer fill up.
63.179: The rare skills a trainee unlocks through Potential Levels can be transformed into her very own powerful <color=#ff9900>evolved skills</color> by fulfilling certain conditions during a Career playthrough. Each skill requires different conditions to evolve, so be sure to check them in advance!
63.180: By spending <color=#ff9900>skill points</color>, you can teach your trainee <color=#ff9900>skills</color> which will activate during races. Some skills only activate if your trainee is using a particular <color=#ff9900>strategy</color> or racing over a certain <color=#ff9900>distance</color>, so make sure to pick skills that suit her style and distance <color=#ff9900>aptitudes</color>.
63.181: Your trainee may gain <color=#ff9900>skill hint levels</color> from various <color=#ff9900>events</color>, including Support Card and Trainee Umamusume events. They can learn skills at a <color=#ff9900>skill point discount</color> based on their skill hint level for that skill, so be sure to use Support Cards that have the skills you're looking for.
63.182:
There are three main types of skills:
-<color=#ff9900>Unique Skills</color>: Special skills that Umamusume start with.
-<color=#ff9900>Rare Skills</color>: Powerful skills gained through hints from limited sources.
-<color=#ff9900>Normal Skills</color>: Skills gained through hints from a variety of sources.
In addition, there are also skills that can only be acquired through <color=#ff9900>inheritance of certain Sparks</color>.
63.183: The rare skills a trainee unlocks through Potential Levels can be transformed into her very own powerful <color=#ff9900>evolved skills</color> by fulfilling certain conditions during a Career playthrough. Each skill requires different conditions to evolve, so be sure to check them in advance!
63.184: A Spark Research Report, one of the rewards you get for filling the Research Gauge, can be used to <color=#ff9900>add a ★</color> to a Veteran Umamusume's <color=#ff9900>Spark</color>. Note that not all Sparks can be improved this way, and Research Reports don't carry over to the next event.
63.185: <color=#ff9900>Event Boost</color> lets you spend double the TP when starting a Career playthrough to double the amount the <color=#ff9900>Research Gauge</color> gets filled upon completion.
63.186: Reaching the last space in each area will unlock a <color=#ff9900>special video</color> for you to watch. These videos will unlock at 05:00 (UTC) on the first three days of the event and 22:00 (UTC) on the fourth day.
63.187: Show your support for a racer by giving out an <color=#ff9900>Umazing!</color> Your first Umazing! in each block will also earn you rewards.
63.188: The Extra Stage is underway! Watch the main races of both Block A and B as well as any races your team may be running in. Spectating the main races will earn you rewards.
63.189: Each club gets its own <color=#ff9900>Club Card</color> which you can customize and share on <color=#ff9900>social media</color> to show off your squad's accomplishments and attract new recruits!
63.190: You'll receive <color=#ff9900>5 League Tickets</color> daily at 22:00 (UTC). Each ticket lets you enter your team in a <color=#ff9900>set of 5 races</color>, each against a different team. Weather and track conditions are randomized for each race, while other conditions are predetermined for the whole event.
63.191: Fill the <color=#ff9900>Hero Gauge</color> by competing in races to gain a <color=#ff9900>Hero Skill</color> when it's maxed out! These skills last for <color=#ff9900>one 5-race set</color>, and some have matching items which can guarantee their activation conditions.
63.192: Race your way up through 16 <color=#ff9900>League Ranks</color>, from Bronze 1 to Platinum 4. Reaching Platinum 1 unlocks an <color=#ff9900>event-exclusive title</color> honoring one of your team members—you can choose from among eligible runners.
63.193: The <color=#ff9900>top 96</color> teams in the League Score rankings advance to the Extra Stage, where a total of <color=#ff9900>24 races</color> split between two blocks are held. All runners who compete will earn the <color=#ff9900>Superhero</color> epithet! You can also opt out of the Extra Stage from the event options menu.
63.194: Show your support for a racer in the Extra Stage by giving out an <color=#ff9900>Umazing!</color> You can give out one per race, and doing so will also earn you rewards.
63.195: In both Block A and B, <color=#ff9900>race 11</color> counts as the <color=#ff9900>main race</color>. The main race of Block A features the teams ranked 1st to 4th, while that of Block B features those ranked 5th to 8th. Spectating a main race will earn you rewards, and you can also watch any of the 24 races from the perspective of any runner you want.
63.199: Club Cards can only be edited by the <color=#ff9900>club leader</color>, who can customize the info and message shown, as well as pick the perfect picture to entice new recruits.
63.211: Once per day, after completing 10 Scout Races, a <color=#ff9900>Special Race</color> will occur. These feature different opponents from the usual races and don't consume Scout Tickets. Winning one will also net you more Scout Points!
63.289: The Uma Jukebox lets you <color=#ff9900>change the background music</color> to any song you've unlocked. Select the jukebox from the menu on the right side of the screen to play songs anywhere you like, whether it's the Home screen, a Career scenario, or any other part of the game.
63.276: The Guided Career option is available for this scenario. To use it, tap the AUTO button on the Career screen and then select a Guidance. Each Guidance can be customized. Once selected, the Career will progress automatically according to the Guidance. Try out a Guided Career and breeze through training!
63.277: The Guided Career option is available for this scenario. To use it, tap the AUTO button on the Career screen and then select a Guidance. Each Guidance can be customized. Once selected, the Career will progress automatically according to the Guidance. Try out a Guided Career and breeze through training!

401.10001: Darley Arabian is the embodiment of courage. She was created in the virtual world by AI that adopted the name of the Three Goddesses, the predecessors to all Umamusume. Although she is free and uninhibited, she is always level-headed. She wishes for nothing more than the well-being of all Umamusume-kind.
401.10002: Godolphin Barb is the embodiment of love. She was created in the virtual world by AI that adopted the name of the Three Goddesses, the predecessors to all Umamusume. She treasures all of creation and watches over it with kindness. Although at times she tests racers to their limits, she does so out of affection.
401.10003: Byerley Turk is the embodiment of discipline. She was created in the virtual world by AI that adopted the name of the Three Goddesses, the predecessors to all Umamusume. Despite her commanding nature, her strictness comes from a good place. She believes that the way to carve out the future is through physical strength.

Although she is free and uninhibited, she is always level-headed. She wishes for nothing more than the well-being of all Umamusume-kind.
